label: NASA 1979 Reference System Study
claim_scope: Establishes the original system architecture for satellite solar power, including microwave power transmission frequency, orbital parameters, ground receiver (rectenna) design, and initial mass and cost estimates. Defines the baseline reference configuration that subsequent studies have built on or revised.
verification_posture: Peer-reviewed institutional publication from a national space agency. Content is historically authoritative but dated — all specifications reflect 1979 technology assumptions and must be framed as historical baseline, not current feasibility confirmation.
boundary_notes: Does not validate current technical feasibility. Predates modern phased-array transmitters, thin-film solar cells, significant launch cost reductions, and in-space assembly robotics. Must be cited as historical reference with explicit qualification that assumptions have changed materially since 1979.

label: ESA SOLARIS Concurrent Design Facility Assessment 2022
claim_scope: Documents ESA's internal assessment of SBSP feasibility under the SOLARIS initiative, covering system architecture options, wireless power transmission approaches, required technology development, cost estimates, and recommended further study phases.
verification_posture: Internal feasibility study from a major space agency. Represents institutional interest and structured technical assessment, not a funded development programme or confirmed deployment plan.
boundary_notes: SOLARIS is a feasibility study phase, not an approved programme. Does not confirm commercial deployment, operational system, or funded mission. Timelines cited are study assumptions, not funded milestones.

label: JAXA Space Solar Power Systems Conceptual Study
claim_scope: Documents JAXA's SSPS conceptual studies including system design, WPT experiments, structural concepts, and roadmap targets for SPS2030+ and SPS2050 technology development phases.
verification_posture: Published institutional research from JAXA. Individual experiments (ground WPT demonstrations) are verified. Long-term roadmap targets are planning assumptions subject to funding and technology development.
boundary_notes: Roadmap dates are planning targets, not funded commitments. Ground-based WPT demonstrations validate component technology, not full system deployment.

label: ISRO Space-Based Solar Power Feasibility Study
claim_scope: Documents ISRO's internal assessment of SBSP feasibility and India's potential role in SBSP development, including launch infrastructure considerations and technology development requirements.
verification_posture: Institutional study from a national space agency. Represents exploratory assessment phase — not a confirmed programme.
boundary_notes: Study phase only. Does not represent a funded SBSP programme or confirmed deployment trajectory for India.

label: Frazer-Nash Consultancy SBSP Feasibility Study (UK BEIS, 2020)
claim_scope: UK government-commissioned independent feasibility study covering system options, costs, technical readiness, manufacturing scale requirements, and policy recommendations for a UK SBSP programme. Primary source for UK strategic positioning and cost modelling.
verification_posture: Independently authored and government-commissioned. Represents structured engineering analysis, not a policy commitment or approved programme.
boundary_notes: Cost estimates are modelling outputs with stated assumptions, not procurement figures. Commissioning does not represent a UK government SBSP deployment decision.

label: US Department of Defense SBSP Energy Logistics Study
claim_scope: Documents US DoD and AFRL interest in SBSP as a forward-deployed energy logistics solution, covering military energy resilience applications, SSPIDR demonstration programme context, and strategic rationale for defence-driven SBSP investment.
verification_posture: Defence agency policy and research documentation. SSPIDR demonstration components are verified. Broader deployment intent represents strategic interest, not confirmed acquisition.
boundary_notes: Strategic interest in energy logistics does not confirm near-term SBSP deployment. Demonstration programme (SSPIDR) is technology validation, not system deployment.

label: European Union Space-Based Solar Power Initiative Documentation
claim_scope: Documents EU-level interest and coordination on SBSP across member states, including the SPS ESCAPE concept, EU space industrial strategy, and policy alignment with energy security and net-zero objectives.
verification_posture: EU institutional documentation. Represents policy coordination and research alignment, not a confirmed EU SBSP development programme.
boundary_notes: EU coordination does not constitute an approved programme. Policy alignment with energy goals is intent, not funded project.

label: Caltech Space Solar Power Project Research Publications (Hajimiri et al.)
claim_scope: Peer-reviewed publications documenting Caltech SSPP system design, MAPLE experiment design and in-space results (2023), integrated photovoltaic-transmitter tile development, and ultralight flexible structural concepts.
verification_posture: Peer-reviewed academic publications with in-space experimental confirmation of key WPT and PV tile functions via MAPLE experiment (2023). Results are verified within stated experiment scope.
boundary_notes: MAPLE demonstrated targeted wireless power transfer in space at small scale. This confirms component-level TRL progression, not full system feasibility or commercial viability at utility scale.

label: MIT Space-Based Solar Power Research
claim_scope: MIT research covering SBSP system-level analysis, techno-economic modelling, rectenna array design, and launch cost sensitivity studies. Contributes to published academic consensus on SBSP feasibility conditions.
verification_posture: Academic research from peer-reviewed institutions. Represents analytical assessments, not experimental confirmation of full-system performance.
boundary_notes: Systems analysis and modelling — conclusions are conditional on technology and cost assumptions. Does not confirm commercial deployment viability.

label: University of Cambridge SBSP Engineering Research
claim_scope: Cambridge research addressing large-structure dynamics, orbital assembly, attitude control, and thermal management for SBSP systems. Published in peer-reviewed engineering journals.
verification_posture: Peer-reviewed academic engineering research. Analytical results are verified within stated modelling assumptions.
boundary_notes: Engineering analyses are conditional on design assumptions. Does not validate full system performance in operational conditions.

label: Georgia Tech Space-Based Solar Power Research
authority_role: power systems and architecture research reference
claim_scope: Georgia Tech research on SBSP power system architecture, microwave power beaming efficiency, rectenna design, and system-level cost optimisation.
verification_posture: Peer-reviewed academic research. Results are analytical and experimental within laboratory scope.
boundary_notes: Laboratory-scale results. Does not confirm system-level performance or commercial deployment feasibility.

label: Kyushu University Space Solar Power Research
authority_role: academic WPT and photovoltaics research reference
claim_scope: Kyushu University research contributions to SBSP subsystem development including high-efficiency photovoltaics for space, WPT efficiency improvements, and collaborative work with JAXA.
verification_posture: Peer-reviewed academic research, often in collaboration with JAXA. Results verified within stated experimental parameters.
boundary_notes: Subsystem research. Does not confirm full-system SBSP deployment feasibility.

label: Tsinghua University Space-Based Solar Power Research
claim_scope: Tsinghua University research on SBSP system design, large-structure orbital deployment, and WPT, aligned with China's national SBSP programme strategy.
verification_posture: Peer-reviewed academic publications. Contributes to national research base for China's SBSP programme.
boundary_notes: Academic research supporting national programme goals. Does not confirm deployment commitments or operational system timelines.

label: IEEE Wireless Power Transfer Standards and Technical References
claim_scope: IEEE technical standards, working group reports, and published reviews covering WPT efficiency benchmarks, frequency allocations, safety exposure limits, and system performance characterisation methods applicable to SBSP microwave transmission.
verification_posture: Standards body publications with established engineering consensus. Safety limits and efficiency benchmarks are authoritative within stated scope and conditions.
boundary_notes: Standards apply to defined conditions. Space-to-ground WPT at SBSP scale introduces scenarios not fully covered by existing standards — regulatory development is ongoing.

label: ITU Radio Spectrum Regulations and WRC Documentation
claim_scope: ITU Radio Regulations, World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC) documentation, and ITU-R study group outputs relevant to frequency allocation for SBSP microwave power transmission.
verification_posture: International treaty-based regulatory authority. ITU regulations are binding for member states. WRC deliberations are ongoing for SBSP-specific spectrum allocation.
boundary_notes: Dedicated SBSP spectrum allocation has not been formally agreed at ITU level as of 2024–2025. Current regulatory framework represents a constraint requiring resolution before operational SBSP deployment.

label: Airbus Space-Based Solar Power Concept Studies
claim_scope: Airbus internal and published concept studies on SBSP system architecture, manufacturing approaches, and potential industrial roles for a European SBSP programme, including collaboration with ESA SOLARIS.
verification_posture: Industry publication. Represents company capability assessment and strategic positioning, not confirmed product development or funded programme.
boundary_notes: Industry concept work. Does not represent a funded development contract or confirmed deployment commitment. Cost and schedule claims are company planning assumptions.

label: Thales Alenia Space SBSP Research and Concept Work
claim_scope: Thales Alenia Space contributions to SBSP system architecture, reflector/concentrator concepts, and participation in ESA and European industry SBSP activities.
verification_posture: Industry and ESA-collaboration publication. Represents engineering concept work and industry engagement.
boundary_notes: Concept and study phase contributions. Not a funded product or programme commitment.

label: Northrop Grumman SSPIDR Programme Documentation
claim_scope: Documents the Space Solar Power Incremental Demonstrations and Research (SSPIDR) programme, including the ARACHNE flight experiment design, phased array transmitter development, and in-space WPT demonstration objectives.
verification_posture: Defence contractor programme documentation, supported by AFRL funding. SSPIDR is a funded demonstration programme — not a deployment programme.
boundary_notes: Demonstration programme context. SSPIDR validates component and subsystem technologies, not full SBSP system deployment.

label: Mitsubishi Electric Wireless Power Transmission Research
claim_scope: Mitsubishi Electric published research and demonstrations on high-efficiency microwave WPT, phased array transmitters, and ground rectenna systems, in support of JAXA SSPS programme.
verification_posture: Industry research with published results. Ground-based demonstrations are verified. Space-deployment applications remain at development stage.
boundary_notes: Component and subsystem demonstrations. Does not represent operational space-to-ground WPT at deployment scale.

label: IHI Corporation Space-Based Solar Power Research
claim_scope: IHI Corporation research on SBSP structural systems, large-space-structure assembly, and solar array deployment, contributing to Japan's national SBSP industrial development base.
verification_posture: Industry research aligned with JAXA programme activities.
boundary_notes: Industrial research and development phase. Does not represent a funded deployment contract.

label: Peter Glaser 1968 Science Paper — Space Solar Power Original Concept
claim_scope: The original published concept for space-based solar power by Peter Glaser (Science, 1968), proposing a satellite solar power station with microwave transmission to a ground receiving antenna. Establishes the conceptual foundation for all subsequent SBSP studies.
verification_posture: Peer-reviewed publication in Science journal. Historically authoritative as the origin document of SBSP as a structured concept.
boundary_notes: 1968 concept paper. Technology assumptions, cost estimates, and system specifications are entirely historical. Must be cited as the origin concept, not as current engineering guidance.

label: NASA / DoE 1980 Concept Development and Evaluation Programme
claim_scope: The NASA/Department of Energy joint Concept Development and Evaluation Program (CDEP) that produced detailed engineering studies of SBSP from 1976–1980, including the Reference System and numerous alternative concept assessments.
verification_posture: Government-funded programme documentation. Comprehensive engineering analysis for its era, widely cited in subsequent SBSP literature.
boundary_notes: Late 1970s programme. Technology assumptions pre-date modern launch vehicles, phased arrays, thin-film photovoltaics, and robotic assembly systems. Use as historical baseline only.

label: NASA Fresh Look Study 1995–1997 (Mankins)
claim_scope: NASA's internal re-evaluation of SBSP feasibility in 1995–1997, led by John Mankins, assessing whether advances since the 1979 study changed the feasibility picture. Introduced the SPS Alpha concept and proposed an updated development pathway.
verification_posture: NASA internal study with published results. Represents institutional reassessment at a specific technology moment (1995), not a funded development programme.
boundary_notes: 1995 reassessment. Technology assumptions have continued to evolve significantly since 1997. Must be framed as a historical reassessment, not current engineering guidance.

label: Space-Based-Solar-Power.com Editorial Methodology
claim_scope: Defines the source tier system, claim classification rules, evidence thresholds, editorial posture, and content governance for Space-Based-Solar-Power.com. Governs how all site content is written, sourced, and bounded.
verification_posture: Internal governance document — describes site methodology, not external evidence. Not citable as external authority.
boundary_notes: Internal governance only. Must not be used to establish external claims. All externally facing claims must be supported by institutional, academic, or standards sources.

label: Space-Based-Solar-Power.com Claim Classification Framework
claim_scope: Defines the seven claim categories used across the site: source-verified fact, institutional summary, technical explanation, strategic interpretation, company claim, unresolved scenario, and speculative future pathway. Governs how claims are labeled and bounded on every page.
verification_posture: Internal framework document. Describes editorial practice, not external evidence.
boundary_notes: Internal classification framework. Claim categories are editorial governance tools, not scientific classifications. External claims require external sourcing regardless of category assignment.

label: Space-Based-Solar-Power.com Source Tier Policy
claim_scope: Defines the three-tier source hierarchy: Tier 1 (institutional and sovereign), Tier 2 (academic and technical), Tier 3 (enterprise and program disclosures). Governs which source types can support which claim types and how conflicting sources are handled.
verification_posture: Internal governance policy.
boundary_notes: Internal source classification policy. Does not substitute for actual source verification. All claims must be traceable to appropriate tier sources.
